# Welcome {.unnumbered}

This book is a practical guide for working with data related to the field of
thermal ecology using R. It focuses specifically on the processes and challenges 
you'll face with such data.










**This book was last rendered on 28 April\, 2026.**

## 1. Environmental Data

Learn how to load and clean raw data from own data loggers, access climate databases and microclimate simulations, and calculating metrics of heatwaves.

- Own data loggers

- Climatology database

- Microclimate database

- Detecting heatwaves/cold spells

## 2. Physiological Data

- Thermal performance curves

- Respirometry

- Thermal-death-time curves

- Q10s and rates of change


## 3. Behavioural Data

- Torpor/arousal patterns

- Thermal images

- Thermoregulation


## 4. Comparative Data

- Effect sizes

- Comparative analysis

- Scaling relationships


## 5. Simulation

- Operative temeprature models

- Dynamic Energy Budget


## 6. Other

- Repeatability 


## Acknowledgements

This book was inspired by the [Cleaning Biodiversity Data in R](https://cleaning-data-r.ala.org.au/) book. This book is a collaborative effort by members of the [Thermal Ecology Alliance](https://www.thermalecologyalliance.org/).
